log_snapshot.scope: complete container log through the zero-tolerance corrective 120K context case and last pre-stop health check; the controller log separately proves stop and cleanup

cgroup_event_records.interpretation: These container-log records are startup/cache-control snapshots with zero high, max, OOM, and OOM-kill values. The separate watchdog trace later records nonzero soft memory.high events and is authoritative for the full lane.

selected_context_receipts.interpretation: Exact retrieval passed despite accepted second-order residue. The corrective case ended with a later zero-residue KPI window; this does not retroactively make every forward miss-free.

host_wide_reclaim_counters.attribution_note: These counters are host-wide and are not exclusively attributable to the evaluation cgroup.

memory_watchdog.interpretation: The 128K quality/context lane retained more than 4.29 GB of hard cgroup headroom with no swap, hard-limit, or OOM event. File-cache growth crossed the soft memory.high boundary and produced observable reclaim and pressure; those events are intentionally not reported as zero.

kpi_semantics.window_boundary: Residue and replay fields describe the latest 64-step KPI window, not cumulative correctness.
kpi_semantics.residue_boundary: Residue records second-order misses exposed after mandatory first-order fetch and replay. Under the default continuation threshold, those experts are available to later steps while their current-step contribution remains zero.
kpi_semantics.unrestored_boundary: Zero unrestored experts establishes fetch/store liveness. It does not establish a miss-free forward, bit-identical logits, or identical token streams across boots.

sanitization_note: Topology-specific and private runtime provenance identifiers are intentionally omitted.
